title: Descriptive Statistics & Basic Probability
sub: Central tendency · Dispersion · Bayes · Conditional probability

h: Measures of central tendency — mean, median, mode (merit/demerit)
Box plot / five-number summary construction & interpretation
Bayes' theorem — conditional probability numericals
Addition & multiplication laws of probability
Conditional probability definition & problems
m: Correction of mean/SD when a wrong item is discovered
Measures of dispersion — variance, SD, CV, range
Coefficient of variation — consistency comparison
Arithmetic mean correction after wrong entry
Scatter plot & graphical representations

qs.t: Write the applications of probability and statistics in the field of engineering. Also mention the importance of diagrams and graphs when interpreting data.

What are the merits and demerits of Arithmetic mean? The mean and the standard deviation of 20 items is found to be 10 and 2 respectively. At the time of checking, one item 8 was incorrect. Calculate the mean and standard deviation if the wrong item is omitted; and if it is replaced by 12.

Write any three merits and three demerits of mode. The following data are the temperature effluent at discharge from a sewage treatment facility on consecutive days. Construct a box plot and comment on the information of this display.
What are the merits and demerits of arithmetic mean? Compute the five numbers summary and prepare the Box and Whisker diagram for the following data and interpret the diagram.

Describe the difference between mean, median and mode with suitable examples. The arithmetic mean and standard deviation of 100 items was 40 and 5. Later on, it was found that an item 53 was misread as 83. Find the correct mean and standard deviation.

What is dispersion? Explain what you understand by absolute and relative measures of dispersion. The marks distribution of 100 students of a college is as follows. Find the mean, median and standard deviation of the given distribution.

The number of minutes that a person had to wait for the bus to work on 13 working days are: 1, 10, 13, 12, 8, 2, 6, 9, 17, 30, 5, 4 and 15. (a) Find the values constituting the 5-number summary. (b) Construct a box plot.

Describe the strong and weak points of various measures of central tendency. From the following frequency distribution find the range of income of the middle 70%, the median income, and also find mean deviation from mean.
Distinguish between absolute and relative measures of dispersion. The running capacity of two horses is given below. State which is more consistent and why.

In two companies A and B engaged in similar type of industry, the average weekly wage and standard deviation are given below. (i) Which company pays larger amount as weekly wage? (ii) Which company shows greater variability? (iii) What is the mean and standard deviation of all workers in the two companies taken together?
qs.expr:
Company ACompany B
Average weekly wage (Rs)460490
Standard deviation5040
No. of wage earners10080

qs.t: Two different sections of a statistics class take the same quiz and the scores are recorded below. (a) Find the range and standard deviation for each section. (b) What do the range values lead you to conclude about variation? (c) Why is the range misleading? (d) What do the standard deviation values lead you to conclude?

What are the differences between measures of central tendency and measures of dispersion? The mean and standard deviation of 20 items is found to be 10 and 2 respectively. At the time of checking it was found that one item 8 was incorrect. Calculate the mean and standard deviation if (i) the wrong item is omitted (ii) it is replaced by 12.

Write any four characteristics of ideal measure of central tendency. For a group of 16 candidates, the mean and standard deviation were found to be 20 and 5 respectively. Later it is discovered that the score 32 was measured as 23. Find the correct mean and correct standard deviation.

An investor buys Rs. 12,000 worth of shares in a company each month. During the first five months he bought the shares at a price of Rs. 100, 120, 150, 200, 240 per share. After five months, what is the average price paid for the shares by him?

Write down the significance of statistics in engineering. An experiment shows the height of 51 plants given below. If average heights of all the 51 plants are 40 cm, find the missing frequencies corresponding to the height 30 and 50 cm.
What are measures of central tendencies? Write favorable points of each of them. Calculate approximate measures of central tendency from the following data.
Write down the significance of statistics in engineering and its importance in engineering. Also mention the importance of diagrams and graphs when interpreting data.

Define conditional probability with example. A box containing 5000 IC chips, of which 1,000 are manufactured by company A and the rest by company B. Ten percentage of chips made by company A and five percentage by company B are defective. (i) What is the probability that the chip chosen is defective? (ii) If a randomly chosen chip is found to be defective, what is the probability that it comes from company A?

Write axioms of probability. An individual has 3 different email accounts. Most of her messages, in fact 70%, come into account #1, whereas 20% come into account #2 and the remaining 10% into account #3. Of the messages into account #1, only 1% are spam, whereas the corresponding percentages for accounts #2 and #3 are 2% and 5% respectively. What is the probability that a randomly selected message is spam? If the randomly chosen message is spam, what is the probability it came into account #2?

State Baye's Theorem. If there are three machines producing chips, first machine produces 2000, second machine produces 3000 and third machine produces 5000 chips per day. Past experience showed that they produce 95%, 97% and 99% non-defective chips. If a randomly chosen chip is found to be non-defective, what is the probability that it comes from the (i) First Machine (ii) Second machine (iii) Third machine?

Worldlink, an internet service provider of Nepal buys signal routers from three countries. It buys 22% of routers from America, 38% from India and 40% from China. It is found that 3% of routers from America, 4% from India and 5% from China are defective. If a customer buys one of these routers, what is the probability that (a) It is defective? (b) If the router is defective, it is from China?

Define conditional probability. Two sets of candidates are competing for the positions on the Board of Directors of a company. The probability that the first and second sets will win are 0.6 and 0.4 respectively. If the first set wins the probability of introducing a new product is 0.8 and the corresponding probability if the second set wins is 0.3. What is the probability that the product will be introduced?

If an app developer is building a Flutter-based mobile app that provides news content, and based on historical user data: 40% of users are sports lovers, 35% are tech enthusiasts, and 25% are political news readers, and the developer implements a feature that recommends an AI-generated article summary, then 80% of sports lovers use this feature, 60% of tech enthusiasts use it, and 30% of political news readers use it. (a) What is the probability that a randomly selected user uses the article summary feature? (b) If a randomly selected user does use the article summary feature, what is the probability that he or she is a sports lover?

Define Baye's Theorem. In a factory that manufactures touch screens for mobile phones, production is distributed among three machines: machine A (30%), machine B (40%), and machine C (30%). 8% of touch screens produced by machine A are defective, while 6% from machine B and 12% from machine C are found to be defective. If a touch screen is randomly chosen from the production line and is found to be defective, what is the probability that it was produced by (a) machine A? (b) machine B? (c) machine C?

Describe addition and multiplication law of probability. A problem in statistics is given to three students A, B and C whose chance of solving it are 1/3, 1/4 and 1/5 respectively. Find the probability that (i) The problem will be solved (ii) Only one of them can solve the problem (iii) None of them will solve the problem.

Define mutually exclusive and independent events. In a bolt factory, machines A, B and C manufacture respectively 25%, 35% and 40% of the total production. Among their total output, 95%, 94% and 92% are found to be of good quality bolts. A bolt is drawn from the total product and is found to be good quality. What is the probability that (i) it was manufactured by A? (ii) it was manufactured by B?

State the law of multiplication of probability. An Electronics company has an engineering position open. The probability that an applicant is capable is 0.7. Each applicant is given written test and oral examination. A capable applicant passes with probability 0.9 while an incapable applicant passes with probability 0.4. Find (a) the probability that an applicant passes the test (b) the probability that the applicant is capable given he/she passes the test.

By examining the chest x-ray, the probability that TB is detected when a person is actually suffering from it is 0.99. The probability that the doctor diagnoses incorrectly that a person has TB, on the basis of the x-ray is 0.001. In a certain city, 1 in 10000 persons suffer from TB. A person selected at random is diagnosed to have TB. What is the probability that that person has actually TB?

title: Probability Distributions
sub: Binomial · Poisson · Hypergeometric · Normal · Gamma · PDF/CDF

h: Binomial distribution — numericals
Poisson distribution — characteristics & limiting case from binomial
Normal distribution — z-scores & area (numericals)
Hypergeometric distribution — definition & problems
Random variable — PMF, mean, variance finding k
m: Negative binomial distribution — compare with binomial
PDF/CDF — finding k, mean, variance of continuous r.v.
Gamma distribution — properties & problems
Normal approximation to binomial/Poisson — conditions
Standard normal distribution — properties
l: Properties of CDF for continuous random variables

qs.t: A box contains 1000 transistors, out of which 200 are defective. If 10 transistors are selected at random, find the probability that: (a) All 10 are defective (b) All 10 are good (c) At least three are defective (d) At most two are defective (e) Exactly seven are defective.
What are the chief characteristics of the Poisson distribution? Under what conditions is the Poisson distribution the limiting case of the binomial distribution?
A random variable X has the density function f(x) = k·1/(1+x²) where −∞ < x < ∞. (a) Determine the value of k. (b) P(X ≥ 0). (c) P(1 < X < 3).
If a random variable X has the following probability mass function, find (i) Value of k (ii) P(−1 ≤ X < 2) (iii) Mean of distribution (iv) Standard deviation of distribution.
Define binomial and hypergeometric distribution with highlighting their similarities and differences.
Define continuous random variables and continuous probability distribution with examples. Also write properties of cumulative distribution functions for continuous random variables.

Write conditions for normal approximation to binomial distribution. A metropolitan city installed 2,000 electric lamps for street lighting. The length of life of lamps is normally distributed with mean burning life of 1000 hours and standard deviation of 200 hours. (a) What is the probability that a lamp will burn for exactly 900 hours? (b) What is the probability that a lamp will burn between 899 hours and 901 hours before it fails? (c) After how many burning hours would we expect 10% of the lamps to be left?

What are the parameters of the normal distribution, and what are their roles? If 100 bulbs with a mean life of 12 months and a standard deviation of 2.5 months are installed in a new factory, suppose their lifetimes are normally distributed. (a) How many bulbs will expire in less than 9 months? (b) How many bulbs will expire between 6 and 15 months?
A random variable X has a random variable having the following probability distribution. Find (i) The value of k (ii) Mean and standard deviation of distribution.

Discuss the condition for Poisson distribution. A traffic engineer has recorded that on average 2.5 traffic accidents occur per month at a particular intersection. What is the probability that (a) exactly 2 accidents (b) at least 1 accident (c) at most 3 accidents will occur at the intersection in a randomly chosen month?

Define the normal distribution. A city installs 2,000 electric lamps for street lighting. The burning life of these lamps follows a normal distribution, with a mean of 1,000 hours and a standard deviation of 200 hours. (a) What is the probability that a lamp will burn for exactly 900 hours? (b) After how many burning hours would we expect 10% of the lamps to still be working?

The number of meteors found by a radar system in any 30-second interval under specified conditions averages 1.81. Assume the meteors appear randomly and independently. (a) What is the probability that no meteors are found in a one-minute interval? (b) What is the probability of observing at least five but not more than eight meteors in two minutes? (c) What is the probability of observing at most three meteors in two minutes?

What are the parameters used in Negative binomial distribution? If the probability that a child exposed to certain viral fever will be infected is 0.40, what is the probability that the tenth child exposed to the disease will be the third to be infected? Calculate average number of children exposed to the disease who will be infected.

Define standard normal distribution with probability density function. Also write any four properties of standard normal distribution. Mention two conditions for the normal approximation to the binomial distribution.

The distribution of marks obtained in examination is normal. 44% of the candidates get marks below 61 and 4% of the candidates got marks above 80. (a) What are the mean and standard deviation of the distribution? (b) Find the percentage of the candidates who got marks above 65.
Define Poisson distribution with example. Discuss the properties of Poisson distribution.

From the past record, there is a small chance of 1 in 500 will die due to certain disease. If 200 patients were admitted in a certain hospital, use suitable probability distribution to calculate the probability that (i) No one will die from the disease (ii) Exactly one patient will die (iii) At least two of them will die.

In a certain city the daily consumption of water (in millions of gallons) follows approximately a gamma distribution with α=2 and β=3. If the daily capacity of this city is 9 million gallons of water, show that the probability that on any given day the water supply is inadequate is 4.

The breakdown voltage X of randomly chosen diode of a particular type is known to be normally distributed with mean 40 and standard deviation 1.5 volts. What is the probability that the breakdown voltage will be (i) Between 39 and 42 volts (ii) At most 43 volts (iii) At least 39 volts.

For the case of the thin copper wire, suppose that the number of flaws follows a Poisson distribution with a mean of 2.3 flaws per millimeter. Determine the probability of (i) Exactly 2 flaws in 1 millimeter (ii) At least 2 flaws in 1 millimeter (iii) At most 2 flaws in 1 millimeter.

A continuous random variable X has probability density function defined as f(x) = k·e^(−x/2) for 0 ≤ x < ∞, 0 otherwise. (i) Find the value of constant k. (ii) Mean and the distribution function of X.

An industrial engineer has found that standard household light bulbs produced by a certain manufacturer have a useful life that is normally distributed with a mean of 250 hours and variance 2500. What is the probability that a randomly selected bulb will have a useful life (i) In excess of 300 hours (ii) Between 190 and 270 hours (iii) Not exceeding 260 hours?
Define and compare Binomial and Negative Binomial distribution with similarity and differences.

Define standard normal distribution. Give the condition for normal approximation of Poisson distribution. The mean inside diameter of a sample of 200 washers produced by a machine is 0.502 cm and standard deviation 0.005 cm. Tolerance is 0.496 to 0.508 cm. Determine the percentage of defective washers. Assume diameter is normally distributed.

A quality control engineer inspects a random sample of 3 batteries from each lot of 24 car batteries. If such a lot contains six batteries with slight defects, what are the probabilities that the inspector's sample will contain (a) None of the batteries with defect (b) Only one battery with defect (c) At least two batteries with defect.

The life of an electric light bulb follows Normal distribution with mean 800 hours and a standard deviation of 50 hours. Find the probability that a bulb burns (a) Between 750 and 825 hours (b) More than 900 hours.

An office switchboard receives telephone calls at a rate of 3 calls per minute on an average. Find the probability of receiving (a) No calls in one minute interval (b) At least 3 calls in a one-minute interval (c) At most 2 calls in a five-minute interval.
A continuous random variable X has following probability density function f(x) = 3/4·(1+x²), 0 ≤ x ≤ 1. (a) Find mean and standard deviation of X. (b) Find P(−σ < X < σ).

During one stage in the manufacture of integrated circuit chips, a coating must be applied. If 70% of chips receive a thick enough coating, find the probability that among 15 chips (i) At least 12 will have thick enough coatings (ii) At most 3 will have thick enough coatings (iii) Exactly 10 will have thick enough coatings.
In a normal distribution 31% of the items are under 45 and 8% are over 64. Find the mean and standard deviation of distribution.

Under what conditions can the Poisson distribution be approximated using Normal distribution? Of a large group of men, 5% are under 60 inches in height and 40% are between 60 and 65 inches. Assuming a normal distribution, find the mean height and standard deviation.
Three people toss a coin and odd man pays for the coffee. If the coins all turn up the same, they toss again. Find the probability that fewer than 4 tosses are needed.

Let the continuous random variable X denote the diameter of a hole drilled in a sheet metal component. The target diameter is 12.5 millimeters. Historical data show that the distribution of X can be modeled by a probability density function f(x) = 20e^(−20(x−12.5)), x ≥ 12.5. If a part with a diameter larger than 12.60 mm is scrapped, (a) What proportion of parts is scrapped? (b) What proportion of parts is between 12.5 and 12.6 mm?
What is the difference between Binomial distribution and Poisson distribution? Explain under what condition Poisson distribution approximates to binomial distribution.

In a busy road of Kathmandu there were 500 vehicles passing from 8 AM to 6 PM. It was found that 2 in 1000 vehicles wrongly entered in a one-way. Find the probability that (a) No vehicle enters the one-way (b) One vehicle enters the one-way (c) At least one vehicle enters the one-way (d) At most 2 vehicles enter the one-way.
A random variable X has the probability density function f(x) = kx·e^(−x²/12) for x ≥ 0, 0 otherwise. (a) Find the value of k. (b) Find the mean and variance of random variable X.

In certain factory turning out optical lenses, there is a small chance of 1/500 for any lens to be defective. The lenses are supplied in packets of 10 each. What is the probability that a packet will contain (i) No defective lens (ii) At least one defective lens (iii) At most two defective lenses.
A typist made 2.6 mistakes per page on average. Find the probability that in the page typed by him: (i) there is no mistake (ii) at least two mistakes (iii) at most 3 mistakes.

Define Negative Binomial distribution. If a boy is throwing stone at a target what is the probability that his 10th throw is his 5th hit, if the probability of hitting the target at any trial is 0.6. Also find the mean and variance of random variable.

A particularly long traffic light on your morning commute is green 20% of the time that you approach it. Assume that each morning represents an independent trial. (i) Over five mornings, what is the probability that the light is green on exactly one day? (ii) Over 20 mornings, what is the probability that the light is green on exactly four days?
The distribution function for a random variable X is F(x) = 1 − e^(−2x) for x ≥ 0, 0 otherwise. (i) Find P(X > 2). (ii) Find mean and variance of the variable X.

A heavy machinery manufacturer has 3840 large generators in the field that are under warranty. If the probability is 1/1200 that any one will fail during the given year, find the probability (i) That exactly 3 generators will fail during the given year (ii) That between 2 and 6 will fail during the given year.
Define continuous distribution, whose probability density function f(x) = kx(2−x), 0 ≤ x ≤ 2. Find (a) The value of K. (b) Mean of the distribution. (c) Variance of the distribution.
A random variable X has the following probability density function as f(x) = kx³(4−x)², 0 < x < 1. Find the value of k, using this value of k find mean and variance of distribution.

The daily consumption of electric power in a certain city follows a gamma distribution with α=2 and β=3. If the power plant of this city has daily capacity of 12 million kilowatt hours, what is the probability that this power supply will be inadequate on any given day?
A random variable X has following probability function. Find (i) Find the value of K. (ii) Find Mean and Variance.
A random variable X has the following probability function. Find (i) Value of k (ii) P(−1 ≤ X < 2) (iii) Mean (iv) Standard deviation.

What is the probability that a randomly chosen diode of a particular type is known to be normally distributed with mean 40 volts and standard deviation of 1.5 volts. What is the probability that the breakdown voltage will be (a) between 39 and 42 volts (b) at most 43 volts (c) at least 39 volts.

During a laboratory experiment, the average number of radio active particles passing through a counter in milliseconds is 4. What is the probability that (a) 6 particles enter the counter (b) More than 2 particles enter the counter in given milliseconds.
A continuous random variable X has probability density function f(x) = k√x for 0 < x < 1. (a) Find the value of constant k. (b) Mean and the distribution function of X.

Among the 12 solar collectors on display at a trade show, 9 are flat-plate collectors and the others are concentrating collectors. If a person visiting the show randomly selects 6 of the solar collectors to check out, what is the probability that (i) None will be flat-plate collectors (ii) At least 3 will be flat-plate collectors (iii) At most 2 will be concentrating collectors.

title: Sampling Distribution & Central Limit Theorem
sub: CLT · Sampling distribution of mean & proportion · Standard error

h: Central limit theorem — definition, importance, application
Sampling distribution of mean — enumerate all samples and verify E(x̄)=μ
Sampling distribution of proportion — unbiased estimator proof
Standard error of sample mean & proportion
m: Population vs sample — parameter vs statistic
Standard error formula for finite population
Sampling distribution problems with normal approximation
Verify sample mean is unbiased estimator of population mean
l: Advantages of sample surveys over complete enumeration
Mean of sampling distribution of proportion
Definition: parameter, statistic, standard error, marginal error

qs.t: Define the central limit theorem for the sampling distribution of the mean. Discuss its role and importance in inferential statistics.

The time taken to complete the IOE final exam by a group of students is normally distributed with a mean of 2.5 hours and a standard deviation of 0.25 hours. A random sample of size n=60 is drawn from the population. (a) Find the probability that the sample mean is between two and three hours. (b) Standard error of sampling distribution.
Differentiate between a parameter and a statistic in statistics.
A population consists of five numbers: 3, 5, 6, 8 and 10. (a) Write down all possible sample size of 2 without replacement. (b) Verify that population mean is equal to the mean of sample mean.

Define central limit theorem and discuss its importance and application. A population consists of the values 1, 2, 4 and 5. (a) List all possible samples of size two selected with replacement. (b) Find sampling distribution for the proportion of odd numbers. (c) Find population proportion and show that sample proportion is unbiased estimator of the population proportion.
Define central limit theorem and discuss its importance and application.

A population consists of four numbers: 1, 4, 8 and 11. (a) Write down all possible sample size of 2 with replacement. (b) Find sampling distribution for the proportion of odd numbers. (c) Find population proportion and show that sample proportion is unbiased estimator of the population proportion.
Define parameter, statistic, standard error and marginal error with examples.

A population consists of four numbers: 1, 4, 8 and 11. (a) Write down all possible sample size of 2 with replacement. (b) Find sampling distribution for the proportion of odd numbers. (c) Find population proportion and show that sample proportion is unbiased estimator of the population proportion.

From the population of size 5 as: 22, 23, 24, 25 and 26. (a) List out all the samples of size three without replacement. (b) Find the sample proportion of even number. (c) Prove that sample proportion is unbiased estimator of proportion of even number, E(p)=P. (d) Also find the standard error of sample proportion of even number.

A population consists of four numbers 5, 9, 13, 17. (a) Write down all possible sample of size two without replacement. (b) Verify that the sample mean is unbiased estimator of population mean. (c) Calculate the standard error of sample mean.

A population consists of five numbers: 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10. (a) Write down all possible samples of size two with SWOR. (b) Calculate mean and the variance of population. (c) Prove that sample mean is unbiased estimator of population mean.

A population consists of the four number 2, 8, 14, 20. (i) Write down all possible sample size of two without replacement. (ii) Verify that the population mean is equal to the mean of the sample mean. (iii) Calculate the standard error of the sampling distribution of the sample mean.

The time at the counter for a customer to be served at a post office can be modeled as a random variable having mean 176 seconds and variance 256. The sample mean will be obtained from the times for a random sample of 100 customers. What is the probability that sample mean will be (i) Less than 177 seconds (ii) Between 175 and 178 seconds (iii) More than 178 seconds?

A population consists of five numbers: 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10. (a) List out all the samples of size three without replacement. (b) Find average of each sample. (c) Prove that the average of sample mean is equal to the population mean. (d) Also find the standard error of sample mean.

If the resistance of resistors provided by a supplier follow Normal distribution with mean 23 Ohm and standard deviation of 5 Ohm. In a random sample of 40 such resistors, find the probability that the mean resistance is (i) More than 26 Ohm (ii) Between 23.8 to 25.6 Ohm (iii) Less than 23.4 Ohm.

The heights of hilly people of Nepal are normally distributed with a mean of 64 inches and a standard deviation of 2 inches. What is the probability that the mean height of a random sample of 100 hilly people is greater than 66 inches?
State central limit theorem. A random sample of size 100 is taken from an infinite population with mean 75 and variance 256. Assert the chances of sample mean between 67 and 83.

State the central limit theorem. A random sample of size 100 is taken from an infinite population having the mean 76 and variance 256. What is the probability that the sample mean will be between 75 and 78?

To illustrate that the mean of a random sample is an unbiased estimate of the mean of the population, consider five slips of paper numbered 3, 6, 9, 12, 15. (a) List all possible samples of size 3 taken without replacement. (b) Calculate the mean of each sample. (c) Verify that the sample mean is an unbiased estimate of the population mean.

A population consists of four numbers 5, 9, 13, 17. (a) Write down all possible sample of size two without replacement. (b) Verify that the sample mean is unbiased estimator of population mean. (c) Calculate the standard error of sample mean.
What do you mean by sampling distribution of a sample mean and its Standard Error? What would be the variance of sampling distribution of mean if sample is taken from finite population?

The monthly income of a particular group of retailers follows a normal distribution with mean Rs. 21,000.00 and standard deviation of Rs. 9,487.00. A random sample of size 10 retailers was taken and the mean income is calculated. Find the probability that this sample lies between Rs. 18,000.00 and Rs. 27,000.00.

The mean of a 200 samples of observations from a normal population with a standard deviation 5 cm is 25 cm. (a) Estimate the population mean with 95% confidence. (b) Estimate the population mean with 95% confidence, changing the population standard deviation to 2.5 cm.

An auditor for a large credit card company knows that on average, the monthly balance of any given customer is $112 and the standard deviation is $56. If the audits 50 randomly selected accounts, what is the probability that the sample average monthly balance is (a) below $100 (b) between $100 and $130?

title: Statistical Inference — Estimation & Hypothesis Testing
sub: Confidence intervals · z-test · t-test · ANOVA · Chi-square · Paired t-test

h: Confidence interval for mean (large & small sample)
Hypothesis test for single mean (z-test and t-test)
Two-sample z-test and t-test for difference of means
One-way ANOVA — construct ANOVA table & F-test
Paired t-test — before/after studies
m: Confidence interval for proportion
Test for difference of two proportions (large sample)
Type I and Type II errors in hypothesis testing
Test procedure steps for large samples
Goodness-of-fit chi-square test
l: Definition: hypothesis, critical value, significance level, confidence level

qs.t: According to the labor statistics, the average weekly earnings of a production worker in 1997 were $424.20. Suppose a labor researcher wants to test to determine whether this figure is still accurate today. The researcher randomly selects 54 production workers and obtains a representative earnings statement for one week from each. The resulting sample average is $432.69. Assuming a population standard deviation of $33.90, and a 5% level of significance, determine whether the mean weekly earnings of a production worker have changed.

Two types of drugs were used on 7 and 7 patients for reducing their weight. Drug A was imported and Drug B was indigenous. The decrease in the weight after using the drugs for six months was as follows. Test whether there is a statistically significant effective to reduce weight Drug A than Drug B at an appropriate level of significance.

During a water shortage, a water company randomly sampled residential water meters to monitor daily water consumption. On a particular day, a sample of 35 meters showed a sample mean of 240 gallons and standard deviation of 45 gallons. Find a 90% and 99% confidence interval for the mean water consumption for the population.

An engineering team is evaluating the performance of three different cooling fluids used in a heat exchanger. Each type of cooling fluid is tested on five samples, and the temperature reduction (in °C) is recorded. Perform ANOVA test to determine if there are significant differences at α = 5%.

In an experiment to study the dependence of hypertension on smoking habits, the following data were taken on 180 individuals. Test the hypothesis that the presence or absence of hypertension is independent of smoking habits. Use a 0.05 level of significance.

Two packaging processes A and B are supposed to fill small boxes of raisins with approximately 50 raisins. Suppose 25 boxes of raisins are randomly sampled from each process and the raisins counted and the following data are obtained. (a) Find sample mean, sample standard deviation for each process. (b) Which process is supposed to be filled more uniformly and why? (c) Find a 95% confidence interval for estimating difference between true means.

A computer hardware analyst wants to test whether processor A is significantly faster than processor B in terms of average processing speed. A sample of 100 units of processor A yields a mean speed of 3.2 GHz with a standard deviation of 0.4 GHz, while a sample of 120 units of processor B yields a mean speed of 3.0 GHz with a standard deviation of 0.5 GHz. Using a 5% level of significance, test whether processor A has a significantly higher average speed than processor B.

A computer engineering department surveyed 300 undergraduate students to explore whether preferred programming language is associated with year of study. The collected data is as follows. At the 5% level of significance, test whether there is a significant association between a student's year of study and their preferred programming language.

A random sample of 500 pipes was taken from a large consignment, and 65 of them were found to be of low quality. Construct the 95% and 99% confidence intervals for the proportion of low-quality pipes in the consignment.
Explain clearly the major steps to be adopted by researchers in testing of a hypothesis for differenced proportions for large sample.

From a lot of units produced by Machine A, a sample of 500 is drawn and tested for a quality characteristic. It is found that 16 units are not meeting the specification. Another sample of size 100 is drawn from the lot of similar units produced by Machine B and tested. In this case, only 3 units are found to be not meeting the specification. Is there significant difference at α = 1%?

The mean weight loss of n=16 grinding balls after a certain length of time in mill slurry is 3.42 grams with a standard deviation of 0.68 gram. Construct a 99% confidence interval for the true mean weight loss.

To compare the effectiveness of three methods of teaching programming of a certain computer, random sample of size four are taken from large groups of persons. Following are the scores in an appropriate achievement test. Test at α = 0.05 whether the differences among the means obtained for the three samples are significant.

A random sample of 200 students was selected and their grading ability in mathematics and interest in engineering were as given below. Is there any relationship between student interest in engineering and ability in mathematics at the 0.05 level of significance?

An engineering researcher wants to estimate the average compressive strength of certain material. Suppose the standard deviation of compressive strength is estimated to be 5 MPa. The researcher wants the estimate to be within ±1 MPa of the true mean with 95% confidence. How much sample should she collect?
If researcher wants to apply chi test for independence of two qualitative variables, what steps she should follow? Write in detail.
Describe the procedure of the test of significance between two means for small samples.
What is testing of hypothesis? Explain the procedure followed in testing of Significance difference between two population proportions for large sample.

It is claimed that an automobile is driven on the average 12000 miles per year. To test this claim a random sample of 100 automobile owners is asked. Would you agree that average miles driven is greater than claimed value if the random showed an average of 14,500 miles and a standard deviation of 8,000 miles? Use α = 0.01.

A study compared the number of hours of relief provided by five different brands of antacid administered to 25 different people, each with stomach acid considered strong. The results are given below. Calculate the F ratio. At the 0.05 level of significance, do the brands produce significantly different amounts of relief?
What is type I error? Describe the procedure of test for difference of two Means for large sample.

A manufacturer claimed that at least 95% of the water pumps supplied to the ABC Company confirmed to specifications. However, the production manager at ABC Company wasn't satisfied with the claim of the manufacturer. Hence, to test the claim, the manager examined a sample of 250 water pumps supplied last month and found that 228 water pumps as per the specification. Can you conclude that the production manager is right to doubt on the claim? (α = 0.01)

In the investigation of a citizens' committee complaint about the availability of fire protection, the distance in miles to the nearest fire station was measured for each of five randomly selected residences in each of four areas. Do these data provide sufficient evidence to indicate a difference in mean distance for the four areas at α = 0.05?

In a recent survey 1,072 Engineers were classified according to their intelligence (GPA in Bachelor) and economic conditions after graduation. Test whether there is any association between intelligence and economic condition.

Four salesmen were posted in different areas by a company. The numbers of units of computer hard disk sold by them are as follows. Is there a significance difference in the performance of salesmen? (ANOVA)
Describe the Hypothesis testing procedure of Chi-square test of independence for 2×2 table.
A random sample of 12 records gives the average of 163.99 minutes with standard deviation of 3.043 minutes. Find the 95% confidence limits for population mean if population consists of 100 units.

The response time in milliseconds was determined for three different types of circuits in an electronic calculator. Using α = 0.01, test the hypothesis that the three circuit types have the same response time.

Four hundred employees of Nepal Telecom are classified according to their level and decisions. Do you agree with the statement that decisions vary according to level of employee? Test at 5% level of significance.

Consider the case of Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Company testing two new compounds intended to reduce blood-pressure level. In groups A, 71 out of 100 animals tested responded to drug first with lower blood-pressure levels. In group B, 58 out of 90 animals tested responded to drug second. Test at 0.05 levels whether there is a difference between the efficiencies of these two drugs.

Shyam and Co. produces three varieties of products: Deluxe, Fine and Ordinary. A recent market survey is conducted for preference of products. Is there a significant difference in the preference of products? Use ANOVA test, α = 5%.

A potential buyer of light bulbs bought 50 bulbs each of two brands. Brand A had a mean life of 1282 hours with S.D. of 80 hours whereas Brand B had a mean life of 1208 hours with S.D. of 94 hours. Can the buyer be quite certain that the two brands do differ in quality? α = 10%.

The following table shows the lives in hours of four batches of electric lamps. Perform the suitable inferential statistics to test whether the different batches of light bulbs have the same mean or not at 5% level of significance.

Bricks made in four kilns have been graded as high quality, average quality and poor quality. The production of bricks (000 unit) in a particular period was as follows. Test whether Kiln and quality of bricks are independent or not at 1% level of significance.

Define chi-square distribution. A book containing 500 pages was thoroughly checked. The distribution of number of errors per page is given below. Using chi-square test of goodness of fit, verify whether the arrivals follow a Poisson distribution at 5% level of significance.
What assumptions are of paired t-test? Write the process of paired t-test.

Is it claimed that a new diet will reduce a person's weight by 10 pounds on the average in a period of 2 weeks. The weights of seven women who followed this diet were recorded before and after a 2 week period. Test the manufacturer's claim at 5% level of significance.

A random sample of 100 men and 100 women at a college is asked if they have an automobile on campus. If 31 of the men and 24 of the women have cars, can we conclude that more men than women have cars on the campus? Use a 0.01 level of significance.

In 1990, 5.8% of job applicants who were tested for drugs failed the test. At the 0.05 significant level, test the claim that the failure rate is now lower if a simple random sample of 1520 current job applicants results in 58 failures.

A study was done to compare the weights obtained with two different scales. Test at the 5% level of significance whether the difference of the means of the weights obtained with the two scales is significant.

The following are the average weekly losses of worker hours due to accidents in 10 industrial plants before and after a certain safety program was put into operation. Use the 0.05 level of significance to test whether the safety program is effective.

Four brands of flashlight batteries are to be compared by testing each brand in five flashlights. Twenty flashlights are randomly selected and divided randomly into four groups of five. Each group uses a different battery. The lifetimes of the batteries, to the nearest hour, are as follows. At the 5% significance level, is there a significant difference in mean lifetime among the four brands?

A computer engineering department surveyed 300 students to explore whether preferred programming language is associated with year of study. At the 5% level of significance, test whether there is a significant association.

A random sample of 200 students was selected and classified according to their grading ability in mathematics and interest in engineering as given below. Is there any relationship between student interest in engineering and ability in mathematics at the 0.05 level of significance?
Describe the procedure of the test of significance for difference of two population mean for large sample.

There was a research on voltage supply by batteries supplied by two companies. Both company claims that they are the same. But researcher suspects there is a significant difference between mean voltages between two companies. To test this, she selected independent samples from both company. Test the researcher's suspicion was correct at 5% level of significance.
qs.expr:
nMeanSample SD
Company A133.59 V0.3 V
Company B103.15 V0.4 V

qs.t: Define hypothesis. Describe the procedure of testing of hypothesis of significant difference between two population means for large samples.

Four trained operators work on production of new product. The productivity of the operators are recorded as below. Using ANOVA, test whether the difference in average productivity due to the difference in operators is significant. Use α = 5%.

In a certain factory, there are two independent processes manufacturing the same item. The average weight in a sample of 250 items produced from one process is 120 grams with SD of 12 grams, while the corresponding figures in a sample of 400 items from the other process are 124 and 14 respectively. Test whether the two mean weights differ significantly at 5%.

A study shows that 16 of 200 computers produced on one assembly need readjustment before shipping while same happens on 14 out of 300 produced on another assembly. Test at 1% level of significance that the second assembly is superior than first one.
In trying to evaluate the effectiveness of antibiotics in killing bacteria, a research institute compiled the following information. Find strength and direction of relationship between them.

A soft drink is being bottled using two different filling machines. The standard deviation of the process for machine A and B was 0.010 and 0.015 L respectively. 30 bottles were randomly sampled from each machine and the means were 2.04 and 2.07 L respectively. Can one conclude that both machines are filling the same volume of soft-drink? Test at α = 0.01.

title: Correlation & Regression Analysis
sub: Simple regression · Multiple regression · Partial & multiple correlation

h: Karl Pearson coefficient of correlation — definition, properties, calculation
Simple linear regression — principle of least squares, fit line
Coefficient of determination — definition and interpretation
Multiple regression — fit equation and estimate
Partial correlation — formula and interpretation
m: Properties of correlation and regression coefficients
Multiple correlation coefficient
95% confidence interval for regression slope (β)
Conditions when two regression lines are the same
Scatter plot construction and interpretation

qs.t: Define correlation. Define regression and write the properties of correlation coefficient. When will two regression lines of a data set be the same?
State principle of least squares. Using principle of least squares, find the possible best fit for the following data set.
Define partial and multiple correlation with examples. Write any two properties of partial and multiple correlation.

Raw material used in the production of a synthetic fiber is stored in a place which has no humidity control. Measurements of the relative humidity in the storage place and the moisture content of a sample of the raw material on 12 days yielded the following results. (a) Fit a straight line by the method of least squares. (b) Find 95% confidence interval for the slope of the regression line of moisture content on humidity.

Discuss the properties of correlation coefficient (r). Find Karl Pearson's coefficient correlation from the bi-variate distribution of curing time and compressive strength (MPa) of concrete. Also compute coefficient of determination and interpret it.

A transportation engineer is studying the factors that influence the pavement deterioration index (PDI) of highways. Lower values mean more deterioration. (a) Find the regression equation of PDI on ADT (Average daily traffic in '000' vehicles). (b) Estimate the PDI when ADT is 32,000.
Define correlation and regression with examples. Write down any four properties of the correlation and regression coefficient each.

A sample of 10 values of the variables X1, X2 and X3 were obtained as shown. Find (i) Partial correlation between X1 and X3 eliminating effect of X2. (ii) Multiple correlation between X1, X2 and X3 assuming X1 as dependent variable.

The following table shows the corresponding values of three variables X1, X2, X3. (a) Fit the linear multiple regression equation of X3 on X1 and X2. (b) Estimate the value of X3 when X1 = 10 and X2 = 8.
Compute the regression line: dependent variable Y on the independent variable X. Also estimate the probable value of Y if the given value of X is equal to 26.
Define Karl Pearson correlation coefficient and write down the basic properties of it. Also define and interpret the coefficient of determination.

A study was done on a diesel powered light duty pickup truck to see if temperature influences emission of nitrous oxide. Emission measurements were taken at different times. (i) Find the mean and variance of the given data. (ii) Find the degree of relationship between them. (iii) Calculate the coefficient of determination and interpret the given data.

An investigation of the relationship between traffic flow x (1000's of cars per 24 hours) and lead content y of bark on trees near the highway yielded the following data. (a) Find the estimated regression line. (b) Compute a 95% confidence interval for the slope of the true regression line.

The following data gives the experience of machine operators in years and their performance as given by the number of good parts turned out per 100 pieces. (a) Fit the regression equation of performance ratings on experience and estimate the probable performance for an operator with 8 years experience. (b) Calculate the coefficient of determination and interpret it.

A computer operator is interested to know data rate of internet users dependent upon band width. (i) Is there any association between band width and data rate? (ii) Fit the regression model and also interpret the estimated regression coefficient. (iii) Compute the band width when data rate is 62.
Fires and Acres Burned. Find the best predicted value of number of acres burned given that there were 80 fires.
Differentiate between correlation and regression. From following data find the Karl Pearson's coefficient of correlation and interpret the result.

The following data gives the number of twists required to break a certain kind of forged alloy bar and percentage of alloying element A present in the metal. (i) Fit the regression equation of number of twists on percentage of element A. Determine the predicted number of twists required to break an alloy when percentage of element is 20. (ii) Find 99% confidence interval for the regression coefficient (slope).

The level of pollution because of vehicular emissions in Pokhara City is not regulated. Measurements of the change in flow of vehicles and the change in the level of air pollution on 12 days yielded the following results. (a) Make a scatter plot. (b) Fit a straight line by the method of least squares. (c) Find the change in the level of air pollution when the change in the flow of vehicles is 30%.
qs.expr:
Change in flow of vehicles (x)283615192418254063121621
Change in level of air pollution (y)22261518211721315281720

qs.t: The simple correlation coefficient between fertilizer (X1), seeds (X2) and productivity (X3) are r12=0.69, r13=0.64 and r23=0.85. Calculate the partial correlation r12.3 and multiple correlations R1.23.

An article in Wear presents data on the fretting wear of mild steel and oil viscosity. Representative data follow, with x = oil viscosity and y = wear volume (10⁻⁴ cubic millimeters). (i) Fit the sample linear regression model using least squares. (ii) Predict fretting wear when viscosity x = 30.

The following table shows the corresponding values of three variables X1, X2, X3. Find (i) Partial correlation between X1 and X3 eliminating effect of X2. (ii) Multiple correlation between X1, X2 and X3 assuming X1 as dependent.
A house survey on monthly expenditure on food yields following data. Obtain the multiple correlation coefficient.

Describe the regression equation of X3 on X1 and X2. A sample of 10 values of three variables was obtained as: ΣX1=10, ΣX2=29, ΣX3=39, ΣX1²=26, ΣX2²=68, ΣX3²=170, ΣX1X2=10, ΣX1X3=15, ΣX2X3=64. Find (i) Partial correlation between X1 and X3 eliminating the effect of X2. (ii) Multiple correlation between X1, X2 and X3 assuming X1 as dependent.
qs.expr: ΣX₁=10, ΣX₂=29, ΣX₃=39 | ΣX₁²=26, ΣX₂²=68, ΣX₃²=170 | ΣX₁X₂=10, ΣX₁X₃=15, ΣX₂X₃=64

qs.t: Fit the regression line of yield of crop ('000 tones) on amount of rainfall (mm) and amount of fertilizers used (kg). Also estimate the yield of crop for the year in which rainfall is 13 mm and fertilizer used is 9 kg.

Following data gives the samples records of the age of cars (years) and annual maintenance costs (Rs. 000). Obtain the regression equation for cost related to age and also estimate the cost of maintenance for 10 yrs old car.

The following data gives the number of twists required to break a certain kind of forced alloy bar and percentage of alloying element A present in the metal. (i) Fit the regression equation of number of twists on percentage of element A. (ii) Determine the predicted number of twists required to break an alloy when percentage of element is 20.

title: Statistical Quality Control
sub: Control charts · X-bar · R-chart · P-chart · Six sigma

h: P-chart (proportion defective chart) — control limits, plotting, interpret
Control charts for variables — X-bar and R-chart
Quality control — importance in engineering
m: Six Sigma concepts
Upper control limit (UCL) and lower control limit (LCL) calculation
Process capability
l: Types of control charts and their uses

qs.t: An inspector selects 10 samples, each of size 200, from 10 different lots of a production process. The number of defective items found in each sample is recorded as follows. (a) Calculate the control limits for the number of defective using a p-chart (proportion defective chart). (b) Plot the control chart, indicating the control limits and points for each sample. (c) Determine whether the process is statistically under control, based on the plotted chart.

title: Descriptive Statistics — Large Dataset Problems
sub: Sample mean · Sample SD · CV · Confidence intervals · Box plots

h: Sample mean, sample standard deviation from raw data
Coefficient of variation — comparing consistency
Confidence interval for population mean
Standard error of sample mean
m: Box plot (five-number summary) from large datasets
Testing consistency using CV
Confidence interval interpretation
Comparing two groups using descriptive statistics

qs.t: Following table shows the number of hours of 45 hospital patients slept following the administration of a certain anesthetic. (a) Find sample mean, sample standard deviation and standard error of sample mean. (b) Construct 95% Confidence interval for the true population mean. Does CI appear that has been precisely estimated? Explain.

The following data show the sales (in Rs. × 1000) of Big Mart across 50 outlets recorded on a particular day: 58, 62, 64, 60, 66, 63, 61, 65, 59, 68, 72, 60, 69, 67, 62, 64, 70, 65, 66, 63, 59, 61, 68, 64, 60, 63, 69, 66, 58, 62, 65, 67, 64, 60, 59, 63, 62, 66, 68, 61, 70, 63, 64, 65, 66, 60, 61, 67, 68, 62. (a) Find the average sales. (b) Is the sales performance of the outlets consistent? (c) What is the total sales of all outlets? (d) If the sales target is Rs. 3,500,000, was the target met on this day?

As part of a study monitoring acid rain, measurements of sulfate deposits (kg/hectare) are recorded for different locations on the Eastern Terai of Nepal. The results are listed for 12 recent and consecutive years. Find sample mean, sample standard deviation and coefficient of variation for sulfate deposits of each location. Give your conclusion about variability and uniformity from the analysis.

A semiconductor manufacturer produces devices used as central processing units. The speed of the device (in megahertz) is important because it determines the price that the manufacturer can charge. The following table contains measurements on 48 devices. Find the (a) Sample mean. (b) Sample standard deviation and coefficient of variation. (c) Standard error of sample mean. (d) What percentage of the devices has a speed exceeding 700 megahertz?

From the following records of randomly chosen days of a departmental store in millions of rupees. Use scientific calculator to compute the required sums. Also compute (i) Sample mean and sample standard deviation. (ii) Test the consistency of the sales. (iii) Also find the 95% confidence limit of sales.

The following table shows the number of hours 45 hospital patients slept following the administration of a certain anesthetic. (a) Find sample mean, sample variance and sample standard deviation. (b) Compare a value that measures the amount of variability relative to the value of mean.

Following data gives the sample records of number of passenger taking tickets at the counter of Bus during one hour period. Find the (i) Sample mean of Number of passenger. (ii) Sample Standard deviation and Coefficient of variation. (iii) Standard error of the sample mean. (iv) Find the 95% and 99% confidence limit of sample mean.

The following data reveals the sample of 22 pairs of observation (X,Y) drawn from large population. (i) Find the sample mean for each variable X and Y. (ii) Which series is more consistent and why? (iii) Find the standard error of the difference of mean. (iv) Find the coefficient of Karl Pearson correlation.

The sample of length of life of bulbs from two companies are given below. (i) Calculate mean length of life of bulbs for Company A and Company B. (ii) Calculate sample standard deviation and sample variance for given data. (iii) Which company's bulbs are more uniform?
List Five Number summary and prepare the box plot for numbers of guest registered each of 60 randomly selected days.

Following data reveals the scores of sixty candidates of IOE entrance examination. (a) Estimate average score of candidates. (b) Find unbiased estimator of true standard deviation and standard error of average score. (c) Also test for consistency of score.

The following are the annual maximum flows in m³/s in a river for a 52-year period. (a) Find descriptive statistics: maximum, minimum, sample mean, range, sample standard deviation, standard error of mean. (b) Find approximate a 95% confidence interval for true average of rate of maximum flow.

Randomly sampled 60 TU graduated BEs, half of whom majored in civil engineering and half in computer engineering. From each, the highest salary offer (including benefits) is stated. (i) Which of these groups shows consistency on the basis of salary offer? (ii) Find standard deviation of difference of average salaries of two groups.

Following data gives sample records of observations on the yield of a chemical process using 3 batches of raw material selected randomly. Construct ANOVA table and test for the equality of the average yields of three batches of raw materials at α = 5%.

The following data reveal the scores of nine samples at entrance exams conducted by three institutes A, B, C. Calculate mean, standard deviation, coefficient of variation and answer: (i) Which institute is good? (ii) Which institute is consistent/reliable?

A study was done on the dependence of hypertension on smoking habits. The following data were taken on 180 individuals. Humidity vs. nitrous oxide data from a diesel-powered light duty pickup truck. (i) Fit a straight line to the given data by the method of least squares and use it to predict the estimated amount of nitrous oxide for 50% humidity. (b) Find 95% confidence interval for slope of line β.
